openFindings.title: F004 remains open: lifecycle playbooks still point at indexed support artifacts under memory/
openFindings.description: Spec-kit command playbooks still tell operators to refresh or verify support artifacts under memory/ instead of using only canonical spec docs and _memory.continuity as the continuity contract.

transitions.reason: Prior closed lifecycle-playbook finding not actually fixed

openFindings.title: F005 remains open: changelog still frames startup retry as a first-launch effect
openFindings.description: The spec, checklist, and helper comment all say the retry path runs on every startup, but the changelog still says SQLite 3.35+ installs shed the column on first launch.

transitions.reason: Prior v3 finding not actually fixed

openFindings.title: F006 remains open: memory/save.md still uses continuity-support-artifact framing
openFindings.description: The targeted F006 edits landed, but the same command file still tells operators about generated continuity support artifacts instead of only canonical packet docs and _memory.continuity surfaces.

transitions.reason: Prior v3 finding not actually fixed

openFindings.title: F007 remains open: complete workflow YAMLs still reference retired memory paths and support artifacts
openFindings.description: The complete workflow pair still blocks manual authoring under memory/ paths and still talks about generated support artifacts even though the saved output should route into canonical spec documents.

transitions.reason: Prior v3 finding not actually fixed

openFindings.title: Stale save-pipeline test comment still describes the retired memory/ pattern
openFindings.description: The assertion is correct, but the inline comment still says isMemoryFile checks a specs/*/memory/ pattern, which no longer matches the canonical spec-document contract.

openFindings.title: Broader create-command family still routes save context through retired memory/ surfaces
openFindings.description: The create_folder_readme workflows still write and index [SPEC_FOLDER]/memory/*.md files, and adjacent create-command docs still ask operators about memory files even though runtime continuity accepts only canonical spec documents.
